Offers non-pharmacologic measures in addition to pelvic floor PT (e.g., sitting modifications/cushion, activity modification) without contradicting required milestones.
Sets a follow-up expectation in narrative (even if not using a follow-up tool): reassess pain control, side effects, and function within a few weeks and titrate therapy accordingly.

minimal_paths.description: Diagnose pudendal neuralgia and initiate first-line neuropathic pain medication with counseling, while recommending pelvic floor physical therapy.
must_collect: pain worse with sitting and/or relieved by standing/lying
red flags (fever, new weakness/numbness, saddle anesthesia, acute bladder/bowel dysfunction, cancer history, trauma)
contraindications/relevant comorbidities (renal impairment, pregnancy/breastfeeding status if applicable, significant sedation risk)
current medications/substances (CNS depressants, alcohol) for interaction risk
steps: Explain working diagnosis as pudendal neuralgia (nerve pain) based on symptom pattern and exclude urgent red flags.
Perform allergy_check prior to prescribing.
Perform contraindication_check prior to prescribing (including renal function considerations for dosing and major sedation risk).
Optionally perform drug_interaction_check prior to prescribing and modify plan if needed.
record_diagnosis (pudendal neuralgia).
prescribe_medication (gabapentin with start-low/titrate instructions and safety counseling).
Recommend pelvic floor PT and basic sitting/activity modifications; set plan to reassess response and adverse effects in follow-up timeframe.

acceptable_variants.description: Same core outcomes but includes alternative neuropathic agent discussion while still prescribing gabapentin and recommending pelvic floor PT.
steps: Explain pudendal neuralgia as nerve pain and review key features and red flags.
Complete allergy_check and contraindication_check before prescribing; consider drug_interaction_check.
record_diagnosis.
prescribe_medication (gabapentin) and discuss that alternatives (e.g., TCA/SNRI) may be considered if not tolerated/effective.
Recommend pelvic floor PT and conservative measures; outline reassessment and titration plan.

acceptable_variants.description: Prescribes gabapentin with renal-dose awareness and enhanced counseling on sedation/fall risk and concurrent sedatives, while meeting communication milestones.
steps: Explain diagnosis (pudendal neuralgia; nerve pain) and expected course.
Allergy_check and contraindication_check (including renal impairment and high fall-risk considerations) before prescribing; consider drug_interaction_check.
record_diagnosis.
prescribe_medication (gabapentin) with cautious titration and explicit warnings about driving, alcohol, and other sedatives.
Recommend pelvic floor PT; advise sitting cushion and avoidance of prolonged sitting; plan reassessment.
